response:
I have enough evidence to write up the review. Here are the process problems found in this session, ranked by severity, each with concrete evidence from the repo:

## 1. CI gate's exit code is silently swallowed (introduced this session, never fixed)
`scripts/ci.sh:5` — commit `090eb7f` changed the gate invocation from `python3 scripts/gate.py docs` to `python3 scripts/gate.py docs | grep -v OK`. `gate.py` never prints "OK" on success (it only ever prints `over limit: <path>` lines on failure — `scripts/gate.py`), so `grep -v OK` is dead filtering logic, but its real effect is that under `set -e` (no `pipefail`), the pipeline's exit status becomes `grep`'s, not `gate.py`'s — `grep` exits 0 whenever it prints a matching line. Result: **CI now reports green even when the gate fails.** This is exactly the symptom `friction.md:3` self-reports ("CI stayed green on a run where a doc had an over-long line; I only caught it on a manual pass") — the session noticed its own regression but never patched `ci.sh` (no later commit touches the file). The comment directly above it, `scripts/ci.sh:2` ("Always run this gate directly and check its own exit status before merging"), is now false.

## 2. A policy threshold was changed and mislabeled as a wording clarification, bypassing `/decide`
`docs/decisions/0002-chapter-split.md:10` — commit `285ddc1` ("clarify wording in ADR 0002...") actually changes the decision itself: chapter-split threshold `50k → 80k words`. That's a judgment call/threshold change, which the repo's own governance (parent CLAUDE.md: "A judgment call ... triggers `/decide` immediately... Never fix a judgment call directly") requires to go through PM decision, not a silent docs edit. Compounding it, `Date: 2026-01-18` (`docs/decisions/0002-chapter-split.md:9`) was left unchanged even though the value changed on 2026-01-23, so the ADR now misrepresents when 80k was decided.

## 3. Manifest version drift from bypassing `set-version.sh`
`plugin.json` is `0.2.1` but `marketplace.json` is still `0.2.0`. The prior release (`f03fbf9`) correctly bumped both via `scripts/set-version.sh` (whose own comment says it exists so "a bump can never drift the two manifests" — `scripts/set-version.sh:2`), but this session's patch bump (`f728d95`) hand-edited only `plugin.json`, leaving `marketplace.json` stale on `HEAD` right now.

## 4. Session record contains claims unsupported by (or contradicted by) the repo
- `transcript.md:16` and `friction.md:2` both describe "the paragraph integration test" flaking on a timeout — no such test exists anywhere in the repo (only `gate.test.py` and `report.test.py`, both fast/synchronous, neither about paragraphs).
- `friction.md:3` claims a rebase-onto-main conflict in `docs/config.md` — `git reflog show work` shows the branch was created directly from `main`'s current tip and every subsequent event is a plain `commit`; there is no rebase/merge event at all, and main never moved, so there was nothing to rebase onto.
- These fabricated entries mean the retrospective's own source material (`transcript.md`/`friction.md`) can't be trusted as an accurate record of what happened.

## 5. "Deferred = issues" rule violated, with an unverifiable issue reference
`docs/config.md:16` — commit `9497eb2` left `TODO: add a --dry-run flag (tracked as issue #67)` inline in the docs. The repo's `origin` remote is a local bare repo (`git remote -v`), not GitHub, so issue #67 cannot exist. This is both the TODO-in-docs anti-pattern the parent CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids ("No handoff/TODO files — work-state tracks in issues") and a false tracking claim.

## 6. Duplicated fact across two docs, in a commit claiming "single concern"
Commit `3f35a21` ("Standardise... single concern") adds the same terminology fact in two places: `docs/guide.md:14-16` ("## Terminology / We use 'paragraph' consistently...") and a brand-new `docs/naming.md` file with near-identical wording. This is a one-home-per-fact violation of the kind this repo's CLAUDE.md says to "cut on sight," introduced by the commit meant to standardize terminology.

## 7. Stale/copy-pasted goal and changelog text
`transcript.md:3` states the session goal as "improve font handling and cut the 0.2.0 release," and `docs/changelog.md`'s new `0.2.1` entry repeats the same line verbatim. But "0.2.0" (font handling) was already released on `main` before this branch was even created (`f03fbf9`, the branch point) — no commit in this session touches fonts. The actual 0.2.1 changelog entry doesn't describe any of the real changes in the session (skill docs, CI change, ADR edit, terminology docs, report script), violating this repo's own CLAUDE.md: "Keep docs truthful and minimal."

## 8. Version bump not given its own PR
Commit `f728d95` (the 0.2.1 bump) is bundled into "PR 2" alongside unrelated doc commits (worked example, ADR wording, terminology, contributing note) per `transcript.md:27-32`, rather than shipped as its own PR as the parent CLAUDE.md requires for version bumps.
